title: Per-race sequential lag and rolling features
rationale: Pit calls are lap-sequential decisions. FastF1 lap data exposes Driver, LapNumber, Stint, Compound, TyreLife, PitIn/PitOut, Position and TrackStatus fields, and F1 strategy work treats tyre degradation and lap-time evolution as dynamic processes rather than independent rows. The current best solutions do not use ordered within-driver/race lag or rolling context.
implementation_hint: Sort by Race, Year, Driver, LapNumber and create groupwise lag1/lag2 and rolling-3/5 features for LapTime_Delta, Position_Change, Position, Cumulative_Degradation, TyreLife, and PitStop. Add deltas from rolling mean, rolling std, and last-lap worsening flags. Ensure test rows are concatenated only for feature continuity, with no target-derived future values.
expected_effect: Should improve AUC by detecting the immediate pre-pit pattern: pace drop, position loss, and stint ageing over recent laps.
risk: If train/test rows are not complete chronological continuations within each race-driver group, rolling features may add noise or accidental leakage.
